slides.title: States of Matter
slides.content:
Everything around us is made of matter. Matter can be in three states:

🧊 Solid — has a fixed shape. A table, a book and ice are solids.

💧 Liquid — can flow and takes the shape of its container. Water and juice are liquids.

💨 Gas — spreads out everywhere. The air we breathe is a gas. Steam from a kettle is a gas too.

slides.title: Changing States
slides.content:
Matter can change from one state to another!

🔥 When you heat ice, it melts and becomes water (solid → liquid).

❄️ When you freeze water, it becomes ice (liquid → solid).

🔥🔥 When you heat water a lot, it becomes steam (liquid → gas).

Heat makes things change!
slides.tip: Ice cream melts when it is hot. That is a solid changing into a liquid!

slides.title: Matter Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn important words about matter:

🧊 ice — frozen water (a solid)

💨 steam — very hot water that becomes a gas

🫗 flow — to move like water

🔥 heat — to make something hotter

🫠 melt — when a solid becomes a liquid

🥶 freeze — when a liquid becomes a solid

🫖 kettle — a container to boil water

slides.title: Changes in the Kitchen
slides.html:
Mum was in the kitchen. She put the kettle on the stove to heat water for tea. Soon, steam came out of the kettle. The water was very hot!

'Look!' said Mum. 'The water was a liquid, and now some of it is becoming a gas!'

Then she took ice from the freezer. The ice was a solid. She put it in a glass. Slowly, the ice started to melt.

'The ice was a solid, but now it is a liquid!' said Tom. 'Matter is amazing!'

slides.title: Simple Past of 'To Be'
slides.content:
The verb to be in the past has two forms:

👤 was — for I, he, she, it:
I was happy. She was at school. It was cold.

👥 were — for you, we, they:
You were tall. We were friends. They were at the park.
slides.tip: was = singular (one person/thing). were = plural (more than one) or 'you'.

slides.title: Was or Were?
slides.problem:
Complete with was or were:

1. The water ___ a solid. Now it is a liquid.
2. The ice pops ___ juice before they ___ frozen.
3. I ___ at school yesterday.
steps: 2️⃣ 'The ice pops' is plural (they) → were; 'they' → were
slides.answer: 1. The water was a solid. 2. The ice pops were juice before they were frozen. 3. I was at school yesterday.

slides.title: Negative: wasn't / weren't
slides.content:
To make the past of 'to be' negative, add not:

👤 was + not = wasn't
I wasn't tired. It wasn't hot.

👥 were + not = weren't
They weren't at home. We weren't late.

slides.title: Practice: Was / Were / Wasn't / Weren't
slides.problem:
Fill in the gaps:

1. The ice cream ___ (not) a liquid. It ___ a solid.
2. The children ___ (not) sad. They ___ happy!
3. Yesterday ___ Sunday. It ___ (not) Monday.
slides.answer: 1. The ice cream wasn't a liquid. It was a solid. 2. The children weren't sad. They were happy! 3. Yesterday was Sunday. It wasn't Monday.

slides.title: The Science Experiment
slides.html:
Today in class, the teacher did an experiment about states of matter.

She put ice on the table. 'This was water before I put it in the freezer,' she said. 'I froze it and it became a solid.'

Then she heated the ice. It melted and became a liquid again. 'Look! It was a solid, and now it is a liquid!'

Finally, she heated the water in a kettle. Steam came out. 'The water was a liquid, and now it is becoming a gas!'

'The experiments were amazing!' said the children.

slides.title: Solids, Liquids and Gases Around Us
slides.content:
Can you find solids, liquids and gases around you?

🧊 Solids: your desk, your pencil, your book, ice cubes

💧 Liquids: water in your bottle, milk, juice, soup

💨 Gases: the air you breathe, steam from hot food, the gas inside a balloon

Remember: heat can change a solid into a liquid, and a liquid into a gas!

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🧊 Three states of matter: solid, liquid and gas
🔥 Heat melts solids into liquids and turns liquids into gas (steam)
💧 Key vocabulary: ice, steam, flow, heat, melt, freeze, kettle
⏰ Past of 'to be': was (I/he/she/it) and were (you/we/they)
